 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.2","title":"[FCP3/P6.5] Capture offline and node-local CLI truth scenarios with reproducible artifacts","description":"Background: offline and node-local flows are the easiest places for truth semantics to drift because they often look similar to mesh-backed flows from the CLI surface.

Scope: implement and capture the offline and node-local portions of the CLI acceptance matrix. Produce transcripts, snapshots, and structured logs that show exactly what the CLI knows, what it does not know, and how provenance is presented when the answer is not mesh-backed.

Acceptance: offline and node-local truth scenarios are reproducible, artifact-backed, and explicit enough that future regressions in provenance labeling are obvious.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:59.724139Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-06T04:40:14.766532Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T04:40:14.766382Z","close_reason":"Verified: 4 integration tests pass (offline show + node-local status, both contract and bundle manifest). Prior work by jemanuel confirmed via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["acceptance","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","phase-6","tests","transcripts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:59.724139Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:49.297418Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:49.825969Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2423,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.2","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group acceptance note: the scenario matrix and captures here should explicitly cover offline, node-local, mesh-backed, degraded, and refusal paths. For each scenario preserve command transcript, structured logs, artifact locations, and minimal rerun commands; do not rely on prose-only descriptions if a later reviewer needs to reconstruct what the CLI actually proved.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:16Z"},{"id":2485,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Claiming a bounded acceptance slice under 8jc8f.2 now that 8jc8f.1 is closed: wire the canonical offline and node-local operator-truth scenarios into the existing test_observability bundle contract so their acceptance evidence becomes mechanically reusable. Scope is intentionally narrow: expose the observability helpers to integration tests, then add replay-bundle assertions for offline_show_workspace_manifest and node_local_status_host_admin using the same frozen availability/provenance vocabulary already captured by the operator-truth matrix.","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:17:08Z"},{"id":2486,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Bounded replay-bundle slice landed locally. `crates/fwc/src/lib.rs` now re-exports `test_observability` so integration tests can reuse the shared bundle contract, and `crates/fwc/tests/cual_integration.rs` now includes helper assertions plus two new acceptance tests that project the canonical offline and node-local truth scenarios into replayable bundle manifests. The new tests assert that the generated manifest preserves the frozen availability/provenance markers, expected phase (`offline-artifact` vs host discovery), and required artifact names from the operator-truth matrix. Local validation is clean (`rustfmt --check` on touched files and `git diff --check`). Offloaded verification is still operationally blocked: both `rch exec -- cargo test ... replayable_bundle_manifest` invocations stayed in 188-root sync planning for the full workspace rather than reaching cargo quickly, so there is no trustworthy remote pass/fail result yet.","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:22:28Z"},{"id":2502,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-06: Verified prior work via rch. All 4 integration tests pass: operator_truth_fixture_offline_show_matches_cli_contract (ok), operator_truth_fixture_offline_show_exports_replayable_bundle_manifest (ok), operator_truth_fixture_node_local_status_matches_cli_contract (ok), operator_truth_fixture_node_local_status_exports_replayable_bundle_manifest (ok). Additionally, 78 truth module tests cover offline and node-local scenarios with acceptance-level assertions.","created_at":"2026-04-06T04:40:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.3","title":"[FCP3/P6.5] Capture mesh-backed, degraded, and refusal CLI truth scenarios","description":"Background: mesh-backed and degraded scenarios are where the new architecture either proves itself or overclaims. They need their own artifact pack rather than being treated as an extension of local-only testing.

Scope: implement and capture the mesh-backed, degraded, and truthful-refusal portions of the CLI acceptance matrix. Show not just successful mesh-backed answers but also degraded visibility, partial evidence, and cases where the system must explicitly refuse to overclaim.

Acceptance: mesh-backed truth semantics are exercised under both healthy and unhealthy conditions, and the resulting artifacts are strong enough for later cutover proof and documentation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:55:00.330586Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-06T05:29:38.444058Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T05:29:38.443854Z","close_reason":"Verified: 5 integration tests pass (mesh-backed, degraded, refusal). Prior work confirmed via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["acceptance","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","phase-6","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:55:00.330586Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:50.466302Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-kpj16.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:51.041172Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:51.643596Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2424,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.3","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group acceptance note: the scenario matrix and captures here should explicitly cover offline, node-local, mesh-backed, degraded, and refusal paths. For each scenario preserve command transcript, structured logs, artifact locations, and minimal rerun commands; do not rely on prose-only descriptions if a later reviewer needs to reconstruct what the CLI actually proved.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:16Z"},{"id":2492,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Taking a bounded follow-on slice that reuses the new bundle-helper layer from 8jc8f.2: add replay-bundle assertions for the canonical mesh-backed, degraded, and refusal operator-truth scenarios already covered by cual_integration. Goal is to make the `.3` acceptance surfaces mechanically reusable without waiting for broader runtime/dependency cleanup.","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:27:39Z"},{"id":2494,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Second bounded slice landed locally. `cual_integration` now projects the canonical mesh-backed, degraded, and refusal scenarios into replayable bundle manifests using the same helper layer introduced for 8jc8f.2, so `.3` truth modes now freeze availability, phase, provenance markers, and required artifact names under the shared `e2e:cli_truth:*` route as well. Local static validation is clean (`rustfmt --check`, `git diff --check`, `jq empty`). I have not claimed remote cargo success because `rch` remains stuck in full-workspace sync planning rather than reaching cargo quickly.","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:29:44Z"},{"id":2503,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-06: Verified prior work via rch. All 5 integration tests pass: mesh_backed_availability_matches_cli_contract (ok), mesh_backed_exports_replayable_bundle_manifest (ok), degraded_health_matches_cli_contract (ok), degraded_exports_replayable_bundle_manifest (ok), refusal_exports_replayable_bundle_manifest (ok). Additionally 78 truth module tests cover mesh-backed, degraded, and refusal scenarios with evidence and provenance assertions.","created_at":"2026-04-06T05:29:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8ky92","title":"TEST-FIRECRAWL: reject ambiguous base_url components","description":"Apply the real-service HTTP allowlist testing posture to Firecrawl: direct API-key mode already pins api.firecrawl.dev or localhost, but base_url validation lacks explicit userinfo/query/fragment regression coverage. Add focused tests and fail-closed validation so authenticated Firecrawl requests cannot carry ambiguous URL components through the configured base_url.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T18:54:39.570542Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T18:59:10.970631Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T18:59:10.969885Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests"],"comments":[{"id":2959,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8ky92","author":"jemanuel","text":"a710ab57 implements Firecrawl base_url fail-closed handling for userinfo, query, and fragment components; verification: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod3-47641 cargo test -p fcp-firecrawl (56 passed), cargo check -p fcp-firecrawl passed, cargo fmt --check --package fcp-firecrawl passed. Package clippy is blocked before Firecrawl by existing fcp-async-core clippy::significant_drop_tightening under -D warnings.","created_at":"2026-04-27T18:59:07Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8oe","title":"fcp.hubspot: Email tracking and sequences","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:15:53.625788349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T17:31:33.752203399Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T17:31:33.752203399Z","close_reason":"CONSOLIDATED: All HubSpot sub-features merged into comprehensive parent connector bead flywheel_connectors-hy8v (fcp.hubspot: HubSpot CRM & Marketing Platform Connector)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8qx","title":"fcp.reddit: Moderation Actions","description":"# fcp.reddit: Moderation Actions (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose a tightly-controlled moderation surface for moderators:
- approve/remove content
- manage mod queue
- ban/mute users (optional)

These actions are high-blast-radius and must be policy + approval gated.

## Scope
- List modqueue/reports.
- Approve/remove a post/comment.
- Optional: ban user (requires strict policy).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.mod.read`
- `reddit.mod.write` (dangerous)
- Optional: `reddit.mod.ban` (critical)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For any moderation write:
  - require ApprovalToken when policy dictates (recommended default)
  - receipts + audit events include thing_id + action + reason (sanitized)
- Avoid logging raw user content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.mod.queue.list`
  - `reddit.mod.approve`
  - `reddit.mod.remove`
  - Optional: `reddit.mod.ban_user`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission
  - forbidden/permission errors

## Acceptance criteria
- Moderation actions are mechanically authorized and auditable.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:22.049835032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:59:39.693446Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:57:53.710446Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8qx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-xz6e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8qx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-xz6e.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1366,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8qx","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented moderation.rs: ModAction (12 types), BanRequest with duration validation, FlairRequest, ModPolicy with protected users and approval gating. 154 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:57:48Z"},{"id":1370,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8qx","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented mod.queue (list modqueue) and mod.approve. Combined with existing mod_remove, full moderation surface. Reddit at 22 ops, golden vectors verified.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:59:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","title":"[MOR/C1.1] Implement revocation check-use atomicity via RevocationSeal pattern","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:31:01.347311Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T00:28:46.041964Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T00:28:46.041202Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0-critical","revocation","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-70qcc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:32:41.418240Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ky3yv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:39.593999Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2627,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Implement a RevocationSeal type returned by RevocationRegistry::check_with_seal() that binds the revocation check result to a specific revocation_head_seq at check time. The seal must be re-validated at operation commit time. If the seal is stale (head_seq advanced since check), the operation must be re-checked or aborted. WHY: H2 Adversarial found a TOCTOU vulnerability ranked #1 CRITICAL: revocation check passes at T1, revocation is inserted at T2, operation executes at T3 against a revoked token. The seal pattern converts this into: check at T1 returns seal(head_seq=N), operation executes, at commit time seal is re-validated against current head_seq. If head_seq advanced, re-check or abort. WHERE: crates/fcp-core/src/revocation.rs — add RevocationSeal struct { checked_at: u64, head_seq: u64, token_id: ObjectId, decision: RevocationDecision }. Add check_with_seal() to RevocationRegistry trait. Add validate_seal() that compares seal.head_seq against current frontier. Require CapabilityVerified to carry the seal. ACCEPTANCE: (1) RevocationSeal type exists with checked_at, head_seq, token_id, decision. (2) check_with_seal() returns RevocationSeal alongside the decision. (3) validate_seal() rejects stale seals when head_seq has advanced. (4) CapabilityVerified carries revocation_seal field. (5) At least 5 tests: fresh seal passes, stale seal triggers re-check, concurrent revocation detected, seal with wrong token_id rejected, seal serialization round-trip. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 2-3 days. DESIGN NOTE: The seal is NOT a lock — it is an optimistic concurrency control mechanism. Most operations will find the seal still valid. Only operations that race with a revocation event will need re-checking. This keeps the happy path fast while closing the TOCTOU gap.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:32:26Z"},{"id":2647,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","author":"jemanuel","text":"CROSS-EPIC DEP: Depends on C3.1 (phantom tokens) because RevocationSeal is carried inside CapabilityVerified, which is the result of CapabilityToken<Verified>::verify(). The seal pattern and phantom token pattern must be designed together so that verify() returns both the Verified token AND the revocation seal in one atomic step.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:40Z"},{"id":2671,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","author":"jemanuel","text":"C1.1 done: RevocationSeal, RevocationDecision, SealValidation types + check_with_seal/validate_seal on RevocationRegistry. 6 acceptance tests, clippy clean. Commit 73832f34.","created_at":"2026-04-11T00:28:45Z"}]}
